page: Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and features a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 168.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25% tier. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, align with average tier results, reflecting the physical core count of four. Memory bandwidth tests show 690.6 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 65.71 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-model LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff, with medium-model text generation falling into the bottom 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8ms_v2 offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a specialized resource allocation for memory-bound tasks rather than raw multi-threaded compute scaling. The combination of high clock speed, large L3 cache, and substantial RAM capacity makes this server suitable for memory-intensive databases, text processing, single-threaded execution pipelines, and workloads requiring premium storage attachments without the need for local disk space or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and 168.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10% tier, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks and medium-model LLM text generation show average to weak performance, reflecting the physical core limitation. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound, single-thread-heavy applications. It is best suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and workloads requiring high memory capacity per vCPU.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 168 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core benchmark performance, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring high memory density, a fast clock speed, and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
